scheme_name: Gujarat Pre-Matric Scholarship Scheme for Standard 1st to 10th
page_title: Gujarat Pre-Matric Scholarship Scheme for Standard 1st to 10th

details: The scheme "Gujarat Pre-Matric Scholarship Scheme for Standard 1st to 10th" by the Tribal Development Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to provide financial assistance for educational development to Scheduled Tribe students studying in Classes 1st to 10th. Through this scheme, scholarships are annually provided to boys, girls, and extremely backward students from Classes 1st to 10th.
benefits: The scheme provides financial assistance in the form of scholarships. The amounts are as follows:
Class 1 to Class 8: Annual scholarship of ₹750/-.
Class 9 to Class 10: Annual scholarship of ₹1,000/-.
Class 1 to Class 5: Annual scholarship of ₹750/-.
Class 6 to Class 10: Annual scholarship of ₹1,000/-.
Class 1 to Class 8: Annual scholarship of ₹1,000/-.
Class 9 to Class 10: Annual scholarship of ₹1,500/-.
The benefits to the beneficiaries will be disbursed through the Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) method.
eligibility: The applicant should belong to Gujarat.
The applicant must be a Scheduled Tribe student.
The applicant must be studying in Class 1st to 10th.

online: On the home page, click on ‘Register’ and the applicant will be redirected to the registration form page.
Enter your Mobile Number, Email, Set Password and then click on ‘Save’.
After successful registration, the applicant can log in through his/her User Name (Mobile No./Email ID) and Password.
On the home page, click on ‘Login’ and the applicant will be redirected to the Login page using your credentials.
Now, click on ‘Services’ and select "Scholarship Services" from the drop-down.
Now, under services, select ‘Scholarship Services’ and then click on ‘Scheduled Tribe’.
Select the Financial Year for which you want to apply and then click on ‘Change Financial Year’.
Select the scheme, read the interaction carefully, and then click on ‘Continue to Service’.
Fill in the application form completely and upload all the relevant documents.
Now, click on ‘Save as Draft’ and agree with the terms & conditions, and submit the application form by clicking on ‘Confirm & Final Submit’.
Photo uploading for all applicants is mandatory.
Application will be considered complete only when all sections are completed.
In case of Any wrong/misleading information provided in the application shall lead to rejection of the application by the Department Authorities.
All required documents must be carefully scanned as per the instructions given.
Student has to fill his/her application carefully as there is no provision for institution change once the application is submitted successfully.
The unreadable uploaded documents will allow the competent authority to reject the student's application.
Direct Individual submission of this application form will not be accepted in case of students studying inside the state.

faqs.question: What is the purpose of this scheme?
faqs.answer: This scheme by the Tribal Development Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to provide financial assistance for the educational development of Scheduled Tribe students.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to benefit from this scheme?
faqs.answer: Scheduled Tribe students studying in Classes 1 to 10 are eligible for the benefits of this scheme.

faqs.question: What is the scholarship amount for boys in Classes 1 to 8?
faqs.answer: Boys studying in Classes 1 to 8 are eligible for an annual scholarship of ₹750/-.

faqs.question: How much scholarship will boys in Classes 9 to 10 receive?
faqs.answer: Boys studying in Classes 9 to 10 will receive an annual scholarship of ₹1,000/-.

faqs.question: What is the scholarship amount for girls in Classes 1 to 5?
faqs.answer: Girls studying in Classes 1 to 5 are eligible for an annual scholarship of ₹750/-.

faqs.question: How much scholarship will girls in Class 6 to 10 receive?
faqs.answer: Girls studying in Classes 6 to 10 will receive an annual scholarship of ₹1,000/-.

faqs.question: What is the scholarship amount for extremely backward students in Classes 1 to 8?
faqs.answer: Extremely backward students studying in Classes 1 to 8 are eligible for an annual scholarship of ₹1,000/-.

faqs.question: How much scholarship will extremely backward students in Classes 9 to 10 receive?
faqs.answer: Extremely backward students studying in Classes 9 to 10 will receive an annual scholarship of ₹1,500/-.

faqs.question: How will the scholarship amount be disbursed to beneficiaries?
faqs.answer: The scholarship amount will be disbursed to beneficiaries through the Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) method.

faqs.question: How often will the scholarship be provided?
faqs.answer: The scholarship will be provided annually.

faqs.question: When will the revised scholarship standards come into effect?
faqs.answer: The revised scholarship standards will be implemented from the new academic session.
